question: Does the image consist of exactly three distinct panels arranged horizontally side by side (triptych format)?
Are the location names 'Himalayas', 'Andes', and 'Mid-Atlantic Ridge' spelled correctly?
Are the location names placed at the bottom of their respective panels?
Is the image free of any explanatory text or labels inside the panels other than the three specified location names?
Do all three panels depict a clear sea level or land surface reference line?
Do all three panels depict the crust-upper mantle boundary (Moho) beneath the surface?
Do all three panels include arrows indicating plate motion directions?
Does the 'Himalayas' panel depict a thick continental crustal root and high plateau topography?
Do the plate motion arrows in the 'Himalayas' panel indicate convergent motion (pointing towards each other)?
Is the 'Himalayas' panel free of deep ocean trenches or subducting oceanic slabs (consistent with continent-continent collision)?
Does the 'Andes' panel depict a subducting oceanic plate diving beneath a continental plate?
Does the 'Andes' panel include a deep ocean trench and an accretionary wedge?
Does the 'Andes' panel show a volcanic arc on the continental side, positioned inland from the trench?
Do the plate motion arrows in the 'Andes' panel indicate convergent motion?
Does the 'Mid-Atlantic Ridge' panel depict a central rift valley or seafloor spreading axis?
Are symmetrically distributed seafloor magnetic anomaly stripes visible on both sides of the ridge in the 'Mid-Atlantic Ridge' panel?
Do the plate motion arrows in the 'Mid-Atlantic Ridge' panel indicate divergent motion (pointing away from each other)?
